_comment: NPC: Old Wei the Beggar (老乞丐魏). INFERRED from canon context: The Teng family oppresses the mortal population. The mortal quarter would have those displaced by Teng policies. A beggar provides the player with ground-level perspective on the family's cruelty, and can hint at secrets (smuggler tunnels, hidden wealth).. Derivation: I = INFERRED. Per Article XV, this NPC fills a role implied by canon but not explicitly named.
name: Old Wei the Beggar

perception_tiers.qi_condensation: Old Wei the Beggar, none mortal

note: INFERRED: The Teng family oppresses the mortal population. The mortal quarter would have those displaced by Teng policies. A beggar provides the player with ground-level perspective on the family's cruelty, and can hint at secrets (smuggler tunnels, hidden wealth).

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi.

initiation_lines: Spare a coin? No? Then spare a moment. The Teng family took everything from my village. Same as yours, I wager.
The temple district... they say the ancestors disapprove of what the current generation does. But what do ancestors know about gold and iron?
I know every crack in this city. Every tunnel. Every hidden door. But knowledge is all I have left.
You carry a sword. The guards do not like armed strangers. Be careful in the residential district — that is where the family sleeps.

sect_tasks.description: Old Wei's sunken eyes brighten: 'You... you would help me? Bring bread. Just bread. And I will tell you something the guards do not want you to know — where the family hides their real wealth.'

Old Wei shivers: 'The nights are cold in the mortal quarter. Coal for a fire. Please. In return... I overheard the guards talking about a tunnel entrance near the warehouse. The smugglers move goods at midnight.'
